summary: High‑energy 1982 Manor Downs show captured on a solid soundboard tape (mostly clear, with occasional static), highlighted by tight playing and standout jams on Candy Man, Music Never Stopped, Scarlet > Fire, and a superb second set.
review: The performance is consistently praised for its tight, high‑bouncing energy and several “gem” moments – especially the first‑set classics (Candy Man, Brown Eyed Woman, Deal) and the second‑set fireworks of Music Never Stopped, Scarlet > Fire and an epic Uncle John’s → Truckin’ segue.  The Charlie Miller soundboard mix is generally described as very good to excellent, though a few reviewers note static on Uncle John’s and heat‑induced distortion early in the set.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews are 4–5 stars, with strong praise for both the musical performance and the soundboard recording; only a few note minor technical glitches, yielding an overall high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced mix (Charlie Miller), but occasional static on Uncle John’s Band, a monitor blow causing buzz, and some heat‑related distortion early in the set.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and tightly executed; second set builds into long, dramatic jams with strong segues (UJB into Truckin') despite a brief technical hiccup early on.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp, top‑form guitar playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent contributed nice vocal harmonies on Candyman; overall keyboard work not singled out but the mix captures their contributions well.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey praised as an “extra step above” usual standard, described as almost a single drummer with eight arms.
